primary_sources.title: RFC 3339: Date and Time on the Internet: Timestamps
primary_sources.design_use: unambiguous UTC timestamps for observedAt, validUntil, and evaluatedAt

primary_sources.title: Verifiable Credential Data Integrity 1.0
primary_sources.design_use: separate proof metadata and temporal bounds from truth of the underlying claim

primary_sources.title: PROV-O: The PROV Ontology
primary_sources.design_use: treat generation and observation times as provenance attributes rather than claim truth

finding: Immutable provenance and trusted-builder acceptance are insufficient for time-sensitive evidence. Evidence acceptance also requires an explicit deny-by-default temporal policy over observation time, maximum age, future clock skew, and validity expiry.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical planned files
coverage: expired and inverted validity windows rejected

trust_limit: Temporal validity does not prove that evidence, provenance, signatures, builders, deployments, runtime observations, or underlying claims are true.
falsification_route: Advance evaluatedAt beyond maxAgeMs or validUntil, move observedAt beyond allowed future skew, omit validUntil, or provide a non-UTC timestamp; the evaluator must reject the envelope with a deterministic reason code.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health material, credentials, personal identifiers, diagnosis, or treatment claims are included.
handoff: Cloudflare and Vercel evidence pipelines should apply freshness evaluation after structural validation and provenance/trust checks, using claim-specific validity windows rather than one global duration.
